blockers.description: User has identified significant and minor deviations from original plan: requirements were shifted, softened, and altered across phases 1-4. A multi-step review/plan/revise/update process is required before Phase 5 can begin.
blockers.workaround: Do not execute Phase 5 until review is complete.

human_actions_pending.action: Audit phases 1-4 implementation against original requirements in docs/PHASE2-SYNTHESIZED-PLAN.md and ROADMAP.md. Document all deviations (significant and minor).
human_actions_pending.context: User noticed requirements were shifted, softened, or watered down from original intent across multiple phases.

human_actions_pending.action: Decide how to address each deviation: revert, fix forward, or accept with documented rationale.
human_actions_pending.context: Decisions will affect whether Phase 5 plans need to be revised before execution.

human_actions_pending.action: Revise Phase 5 and Phase 6 plans if upstream deviations changed interfaces or invariants they depend on.
human_actions_pending.context: Phase 5 (PPMI-weighted scoring, exploration injection) may have different assumptions if Phases 1-4 were implemented differently than planned.

decisions.decision: SHA-256 over MD5 for canary bucket hashing
decisions.rationale: Security scanner blocked MD5; distribution properties identical for A/B use

decisions.decision: shadow mode default returns all tools — 16 existing pipeline tests updated to rollout_stage=ga to preserve their filtering intent
decisions.rationale: Shadow=passthrough is backward compatible; tests had implicit filtering expectations that required explicit ga stage after the routing change

decisions.decision: is_filtered flag gates bounded active set vs passthrough in get_tools_for_list
decisions.rationale: Single branch point for canary routing; replaces the old enabled-only check with stage-aware dispatch

next_action: DO NOT run /gsd:execute-phase 5. Instead: audit phases 1-4 against original requirements in docs/PHASE2-SYNTHESIZED-PLAN.md to enumerate all deviations. Then plan remediation before executing Phase 5.
context_notes: Phase 4 executed and verified cleanly (14/14 must-haves, 975 tests passing). The blocker is not a technical failure but a requirements alignment concern raised by the user: implementations across phases may have drifted from the original synthesized plan. The most notable known deviation from this session: 16 pre-existing pipeline tests were changed from their original rollout_stage expectations during 04-03 to accommodate the new canary routing logic — the user may consider this an unauthorized modification of test contracts.
